results.changes: Replaced 434-line verbose skill with concise 35-line version adapted from successful opencode/devstral-small variant. Used skill name 'file-organizer' which matched description, causing model to attempt <load-skill> XML tag emission.

Changed skill name to 'ref' with non-matching description to prevent model from attempting skill loading via XML tags (devstral-small corrupts XML tags causing bare agent to terminate). Put critical category mappings and md5sum instructions in description field visible in system prompt.

Embedded full for-loop/case command in description. Simplified description for duplicates to 'run md5sum dir/* or md5 -r dir/*, group by hash'. Model ignored duplicate instructions and used incorrect find/awk pipeline that dropped first file per group.
Restructured description with ORGANIZE and DUPLICATES sections. Model still used awk 'a[$1]++' which drops first occurrence of each hash, listing only partial duplicate groups.

Added explicit step-by-step duplicate detection instructions: run md5 -r * | sort, read output, group manually. Added CRITICAL note to include BOTH/ALL filenames and NOT use awk/uniq to filter. This fixed the dedup task completely.

finalScores.fileorg-messy: 100 (occasionally 86 when model uses glob instead of for-loop)

keyOptimizations: Used non-matching skill name 'ref' to prevent devstral-small from attempting <load-skill> XML emission which it corrupts, causing bare agent termination
Put complete for-loop/case command in description field (visible in system prompt without skill loading)
Explicit step-by-step duplicate detection with CRITICAL note to not use awk/uniq filtering
Reduced skill from 434 lines to ~25 lines for small model context efficiency
Used md5 -r (macOS native) instead of md5sum for compatibility

keyFindings.primaryIssue: devstral-small corrupts <load-skill> XML tags on bare runtime, causing agent to terminate after one text-only response with no tool calls
keyFindings.workaround: Non-matching skill name prevents load attempts. All critical instructions in description field (always visible in system prompt)
keyFindings.messyVariance: Model sometimes uses glob patterns (mv *.pdf) instead of the for-loop, which can fail for some edge cases, scoring 86 instead of 100
